goal: B2-5 reversed cards get a distinct glowing treatment in the reading result
done: buildCardArt (the single chokepoint for every result card image — daily hero, question, Celtic slots) adds .is-reversed when orientation==='reversed'. CSS gives .card-art.is-reversed .card-art-img a cool-violet glow ring + gentle pulse (mt-reversed-glow, reduced-motion safe). No rotation — the -reversed asset is already drawn upside-down, so rotating would undo it; the glow is the at-a-glance signal.
verified: harness: injected .card-art.is-reversed → computed box-shadow = violet rgba(124,92,214) + animationName mt-reversed-glow (CSS rule applies). Live card render needs CDN art (CORS-blocked on localhost) so on-card visual pending live/TestFlight. node --check passes; md5 parity canonical=www=ios.
